parameters.name: innodb_buffer_pool_size
parameters.description: Size of InnoDB buffer pool. The single most important MySQL performance parameter.
parameters.notes: Set to 70-80% of available RAM for dedicated MySQL servers.

parameters.name: innodb_buffer_pool_instances
parameters.description: Number of buffer pool instances for concurrent access.
parameters.notes: Set to number of CPU cores, max 64. Each instance >1GB recommended.

parameters.name: innodb_log_file_size
parameters.description: Size of each InnoDB redo log file.
parameters.notes: Larger logs reduce checkpoint frequency. 1-2GB recommended for write-heavy workloads.

parameters.name: innodb_log_buffer_size
parameters.description: Buffer for InnoDB redo log writes before flush to disk.
parameters.notes: 64-128MB for write-heavy workloads.

parameters.name: innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit
parameters.description: Controls redo log flush behavior on transaction commit.
parameters.notes: 0=fastest/lossy, 1=safest/slow(disk flush every commit), 2=fast+safe(loss on power failure only).

parameters.name: innodb_flush_method
parameters.description: I/O flush method for InnoDB data and log files.
parameters.notes: O_DIRECT recommended on Linux to avoid double-buffering.

parameters.name: innodb_io_capacity
parameters.description: InnoDB I/O capacity limit per second for background tasks.
parameters.notes: Set to SSD IOPS capability (e.g. 2000-5000 for modern SSDs).

parameters.name: innodb_io_capacity_max
parameters.description: Maximum I/O capacity InnoDB can use in emergency situations.
parameters.notes: 2x-4x of innodb_io_capacity.

parameters.name: innodb_read_io_threads
parameters.description: Number of background read I/O threads.

parameters.name: innodb_write_io_threads
parameters.description: Number of background write I/O threads.

parameters.name: innodb_thread_concurrency
parameters.description: Maximum number of concurrent threads inside InnoDB. 0=unlimited.
parameters.notes: Set to 2x CPU cores for high-concurrency workloads.

parameters.name: max_connections
parameters.description: Maximum number of simultaneous client connections.
parameters.notes: Higher values consume more memory (per-connection buffers).

parameters.name: max_allowed_packet
parameters.description: Maximum size of one packet or any generated/intermediate string.

parameters.name: thread_cache_size
parameters.description: Number of threads to cache for reuse on connection close.
parameters.notes: Set based on threads_created rate. 100+ recommended for high-connection servers.

parameters.name: table_open_cache
parameters.description: Number of open table descriptors cached per connection.

parameters.name: table_definition_cache
parameters.description: Number of table definitions to cache.

parameters.name: tmp_table_size
parameters.description: Maximum size of internal in-memory temporary tables.
parameters.notes: Larger values reduce temp table disk usage but consume RAM.

parameters.name: sort_buffer_size
parameters.description: Per-session buffer size for sort operations.
parameters.notes: Be careful: per-connection allocation. High value x many connections = OOM risk.

parameters.name: join_buffer_size
parameters.description: Per-session buffer size for joins without indexes.
parameters.notes: Per-connection allocation. Only increase if full table scans are frequent.

parameters.name: query_cache_type
parameters.description: Query cache mode. Deprecated in 5.7, removed in 8.0.
parameters.notes: Set 0=OFF. Query cache is removed in MySQL 8.0 and causes contention.

parameters.name: innodb_adaptive_hash_index
parameters.description: Enable adaptive hash index for B-tree lookups.
parameters.notes: Disable if innodb_thread_concurrency is high (>32).

parameters.name: innodb_doublewrite
parameters.description: Enable doublewrite buffer for page write safety.
parameters.notes: Disable only on ZFS/BTRFS or if performance outweighs data integrity.

parameters.name: sync_binlog
parameters.description: Synchronize binary log to disk after N commits. 1=every commit, 0=OS decides.
parameters.notes: 0 is fastest. 1 is safest. For performance, 1000+ is a good compromise.

parameters.name: innodb_page_cleaners
parameters.description: Number of page cleaner threads for dirty page flushing.
parameters.notes: Set equal to innodb_buffer_pool_instances.

parameters.name: innodb_purge_threads
parameters.description: Number of background purge threads for undo log cleanup.
parameters.notes: Increase for write-heavy workloads with many UPDATE/DELETE operations.

parameters.name: innodb_old_blocks_time
parameters.description: Time in ms before a block can be evicted from the LRU list.
parameters.notes: Higher values prevent table scan pollution of buffer pool.
